psychoanalyst.definition: a doctor who helps people understand their deep feelings and thoughts
psychoanalyst.example: The psychoanalyst helped her understand why she was unhappy.

demand.definition: a strong request that something must happen
demand.example: The demand to always be happy can actually hurt us.

undermines.example: Trying to be happy all the time undermines our real lives.

pleasure.definition: a feeling of enjoyment or happiness
pleasure.example: Life involves both pain and pleasure.

banish.definition: to send away and not allow to come back
banish.example: We cannot banish pain from our lives completely.

numb.definition: to make something so you cannot feel it
numb.example: Some people try to numb their pain instead of facing it.

modify.definition: to change something a little to make it better or different
modify.example: We need to learn to accept pain and modify it over time.

attaining.definition: reaching or achieving something you worked for
attaining.example: Attaining your goals does not always bring happiness.

assumptions.definition: things you believe are true without proof
assumptions.example: Our assumptions about what makes us happy are often wrong.

unconsciously.definition: without knowing or being aware of it
unconsciously.example: We are unconsciously led astray by images of perfect lives.

astray.definition: in the wrong direction, away from what is right
astray.example: Beautiful advertisements can lead us astray.

gleaming.definition: shining brightly and looking clean and new
gleaming.example: The advertisement showed gleaming cars and beautiful houses.

architecture.definition: the design and style of buildings
architecture.example: Pictures of wonderful architecture can make us want more things.

condition.definition: to train someone to behave or think in a certain way over time
condition.example: Advertisements condition us to want things we do not need.

advertisements.definition: messages in pictures or video that try to make you buy something
advertisements.example: There are no advertisements showing people working through their problems.

demons.definition: inner problems or fears that trouble you
demons.example: Working through your demons is part of finding real joy.

spontaneity.definition: doing things naturally without planning, being free and natural
spontaneity.example: Accepting pain helps you find your spontaneity and joy.

universally.example: This truth should be universally acknowledged.

acknowledged.example: This important truth should be acknowledged by everyone.

therapist.definition: a person trained to help others with emotional or mental problems
therapist.example: The therapist helped her understand her feelings better.

fade.definition: to slowly become weaker or disappear
fade.example: You cannot fade out sadness without also fading out joy.

mixing board.definition: a machine that controls the volume of different sounds separately
mixing board.example: Emotions do not have a mixing board to control each feeling alone.

volume.definition: how loud or quiet a sound is
volume.example: When you turn down one emotion, the whole volume goes down.

exposed.definition: shown something or put in contact with something new
exposed.example: Before children are exposed to consumer culture, they know what matters.

satisfying.definition: giving you a feeling of being content and pleased
satisfying.example: Real connection is more satisfying than having things.

caregivers.definition: people who take care of children or sick people
caregivers.example: Children need to feel understood by their parents and caregivers.

equipped.definition: having the skills or tools needed to do something
equipped.example: We are ill equipped to help others if we ignore our own feelings.

consumerism.definition: the belief that buying things brings happiness
consumerism.example: The gods of consumerism tell us happiness comes from stuff.

drilled.definition: taught something by repeating it many times
drilled.example: Advertisements have drilled into us that things make us happy.

skulls.definition: the bones of the head (used here to mean minds)
skulls.example: Consumerism has been drilled into our skulls.

cleverest.example: Happiness is not about being the cleverest or richest.

shiniest.definition: the most bright and attractive on the surface
shiniest.example: Being the shiniest person is not what makes you happy.

relate.definition: to connect with someone, to build a relationship
relate.example: The way we learn to relate to our parents shapes all our relationships.

habit-forming.definition: creating patterns of behavior that are hard to change
habit-forming.example: How we relate to our parents is habit-forming.

blueprint.definition: a plan or model that other things are based on
blueprint.example: Our early relationships are a blueprint for later ones.

groove.definition: a pattern or routine that is hard to break out of
groove.example: We can get into a groove of always needing to be right.

dynamics.definition: the way people or things interact and affect each other
dynamics.example: Unhealthy dynamics can limit our capacity for happiness.

brake.definition: something that slows down or stops progress
brake.example: These patterns put a brake on developing closeness.

aptitude.definition: a natural ability to do something well
aptitude.example: Hiding feelings can limit our aptitude for intimacy.

intimacy.definition: a close, warm relationship with deep understanding between people
intimacy.example: True intimacy requires being honest about your feelings.

hairdressing.definition: the business of cutting and styling hair
hairdressing.example: She runs a hairdressing business in the city.

revelled.example: She revelled in being told she was a big sister.

novelty.definition: the quality of being new and exciting
novelty.example: The novelty of having a new baby wore thin quickly.

tantrums.definition: sudden bursts of anger, usually by a child
tantrums.example: She began having more tantrums and refusing to cooperate.

cooperate.definition: to work together and do what is asked
cooperate.example: She was refusing to cooperate with anything.

wetting.definition: making wet with urine, losing bladder control
wetting.example: She started wetting the bed again.

irritated.example: I can still be irritated by my sister for no reason.

articulate.example: I wanted to articulate her feelings to her.

squabbles.definition: small, not serious fights or arguments
squabbles.example: Although there are squabbles, they mostly get along.

animosity.definition: a strong feeling of dislike or anger toward someone
animosity.example: Most of the time they play without animosity.

empathy.definition: the ability to understand and share another person's feelings
empathy.example: Empathy is harder work than it may at first seem.

material.definition: related to physical things you can own or buy
material.example: Having material things is not the key to happiness.

validate.definition: to show that someone's feelings are real and important
validate.example: Validating our children's feelings strengthens our bond.

strengthens.example: Accepting feelings strengthens the bond between parent and child.

convinced.example: She was not convinced by the present from the baby.

fooled.definition: tricked into believing something that is not true
fooled.example: She was not fooled by the gift from the baby.

nappies.definition: soft cloth or paper babies wear to catch waste (British for diapers)
nappies.example: She started helping by fetching nappies.

persuade.definition: to try to make someone believe or do something
persuade.example: My mum tried to persuade him he did not want to get wet.

disappointed.definition: sad because something did not happen as you hoped
disappointed.example: I told him I was sorry he was disappointed.
